Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: stage top-level dotnet-counters data in experiment commits

Invoke-ScaleTests.ps1 writes dotnet-counters.json and .csv to the
experiment top-level directory, but the artifact staging logic in
Apply-Suggestion, Revert-ExperimentCode, and the amend block in
Invoke-HoneLoop only staged the diagnostics/ subdirectory copy.

This caused counter and efficiency data to be missing from all
committed experiments, breaking the dashboard charts beyond baseline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Updated contributing section with Pester test instructions
- Updated version status description

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix simple mode insurance form broken after switching from advanced mode (#1095)

* Initial plan

* Fix simple mode insurance form when switching from advanced mode

Two bugs caused the simple mode insurance setup form to fail after
switching back from advanced mode:

1. updateTierDrivenAdvancedControls() unconditionally re-enabled
   advanced inputs (advancedPremiumInput, advancedMaxPayoutInput,
   advancedChargeFrequencyInput) regardless of active mode. Since
   setMode() calls this function at the end, switching to simple mode
   would re-enable advanced inputs that were just disabled.

2. The form submit handler also unconditionally re-enabled those same
   advanced inputs, causing advanced values (entered while in advanced
   mode) to be submitted alongside empty simple mode fields when
   submitting in simple mode.

Fix: add early return in updateTierDrivenAdvancedControls() when not in
advanced mode, and guard the submit handler's advanced-input re-enabling
behind an isAdvancedMode check.

Co-authored-by: timwonderer <76604824+timwonderer@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: timwonderer <76604824+timwonderer@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: performance audit and optimization (#247)

- Fix EventBus memory leaks in 6 systems (AchievementSystem, AudioManager, ScoringSystem, WeedSystem, SynergySystem)
- Add bound listener cleanup in destroy() methods to prevent leak of event handlers
- Fix AudioManager ambient timer leaks (ambientIntervals not cleared)
- Implement TileRenderer dirty tracking optimization (only update changed tiles vs all 144+ per frame)
- Add GridSystem early return when TileRenderer active (avoid duplicate rendering)
- Optimize PlantSystem advanceDay() to combine dead plant detection (single pass vs double iteration)
- Simplify PlayerSystem plant lookup (remove nested loop)
- Bundle size verified: 174.87 KB gzipped (target <500KB) — 35% of target ✓
- All hot path optimizations tested and type-checked

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add AdaptiveBatchSizer for dynamic batch size adjustment:

- PageComplexity scoring (area, file size, tables, images, DPI)
- BatchResult with throughput tracking
- Warmup phase before adaptation begins
- Memory pressure → reduce batch, throughput gain → increase batch
- Min/max clamping and adjustment damping
- 52 tests including thread safety
